blocks.content: The children familiarly pushed three tables together.
Altol, seated at the head, placed an order.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: First, a round of beers for everyone, and the usual snacks.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone felt a sense of nostalgia.
Back when they used to hang out, none of them could even touch alcohol.
But now, they sat cross-legged like adults, chugging their beers.
Compared to them, the kids at the magic academy still seemed like children.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: So, Shirone, can you hold your liquor?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Oh, no. I've never actually had a drink before.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: What? So you're still a kid, huh? You don't even have any hair down there yet, do you? Pfft—hahaha!
The children burst into laughter at Altol's joke.
But Lumina, the only girl among them, frowned in disapproval.

blocks.speaker: Lumina
blocks.content: Is that any way to talk to a friend you haven't seen in so long? Honestly, your mouth is as rough as ever.
Her open defense of Shirone made Altol's face flush slightly.
He had known since childhood that Lumina had a soft spot for Shirone.
But now, none of them were kids anymore.
In this world, the strong won the admiration of beautiful women.
Altol believed that as the future village chief, he should be the one by Lumina's side.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Still, his joy at reuniting with Shirone quickly drowned out any resentment.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone, too, was happy to see his old friends after so long.
Even the unfamiliar taste of beer suited him, and the cheerful atmosphere continued.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Hey, remember when Haulan noona tried to jump Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Martin
blocks.content: Hey! Why bring that up now? Cut it out!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Me?

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Yeah. You were surprisingly strong back then. Let's see if you've still got it.
The children pounded the table, egging them on.

blocks.speaker: Martin
blocks.content: Ooooh! Warrior vs. mage! The battle of the century!
The lively atmosphere lifted Shirone's spirits, and he pushed aside his gloomy thoughts.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Arm-wrestling someone who could lift adults with ease? It was absurd, pride aside.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Fine. Let's do it. But no going easy.
The moment their hands clasped, Shirone felt like he was gripping a boulder.
He could use photon magic to flip Altol and get free drinks, but that was just a fun thought.
Martin volunteered as referee.
Ready… go!
Shirone strained with all his might, but Altol's strength surged like a wave, overpowering him effortlessly.
Shirone's arm slammed onto the table, and he tumbled to the floor, rubbing his elbow.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ow, ow, ow. You're really strong.

blocks.speaker: Martin
blocks.content: Wow! Altol's the best! Shirone's a mage, and he got crushed in one go!
Magic and arm-wrestling had nothing to do with each other, but to the children, the title of 'mage' carried significant weight.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Hahaha! Shirone, you've really gotten weak. You weren't this bad before. Well, this bet turned out weird. I'll cover the drinks, Shirone.
Lumina helped Shirone up, her eyes filled with apology.

blocks.speaker: Lumina
blocks.content: Shirone, are you okay? Honestly, Altol, must you be so rough?
As a member of the vagabond village, she knew why Altol had gone all out.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone, familiar with the life of a hunter, didn't take it personally.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's fine. But Altol's gotten really strong.
As Shirone stood, a pouch of gold coins fell from his pocket.
The coins spilled out, rolling across the floor, and the children's eyes widened.

blocks.speaker: Martin
blocks.content: Whoa! Are these real gold coins?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What? Let me see! Wow! Five of them!
Even the kids at the far end of the table crowded around, mesmerized by the gold.

blocks.speaker: Martin
blocks.content: You're loaded! Why are you carrying five gold coins?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I brought extra just in case. There's a book I really want to buy, and I didn't want to be short.

blocks.speaker: Martin
blocks.content: Well, books are expensive.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, I haven't thought about that…

blocks.speaker: Martin
blocks.content: Then have you been to… you know, those places with lots of women? Nobles go there all the time, right?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Well… some do, but not all…
The children's enthusiastic questioning made Altol's expression darken.
It was as if Shirone had become the leader.
The hierarchy in the vagabond village wasn't just about beating up weaker kids.
Without a strong leader to maintain order, people's greed for more would spiral out of control.
For them, isolated from society, discord meant the collapse of their community.
The reason they chose a leader from a young age was to prepare for the day they'd have to take responsibility for the village.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Enough. What's with all the questions? Are you jealous of Shirone's life?

blocks.speaker: Martin
blocks.content: Of course we are. The nobles are giving him money, after all.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: But if you don't become a mage, you're screwed, right, Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Oh, yeah, of course.

blocks.speaker: Martin
blocks.content: Relying on someone else's money means entrusting your life to them. If you succeed, fine, but if you fail, you'll have nothing left. Shirone, you shouldn't do that.
Even if he didn't become a mage, his education would still help him find a job.
As a commoner, there were plenty of places that would hire him if he swallowed his pride.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But it was true that Shirone had never considered the possibility of failure.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How could he be so reckless? Was it overconfidence? If he didn't become a mage, what kind of life would he have?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: His stomach churned. Failure. Everyone could fail, and Shirone was no exception.
The children's practical questions left his heart cold.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Alright, enough serious talk. Hey, Shirone, how about an arm-wrestling match? For old times' sake?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Me?

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Yeah. You were surprisingly strong back then. Let's see if you've still got it.
The children pounded the table, egging them on.
